feed.title: MetaLichen Lab
feed.subtitle: Our work focuses on fungal-algal symbioses, known as lichens. How is it possible that multiple microbes - fungi, algae, bacteria - self-assemble into a lichen, which is big, complex, and three-dimensional? How can symbiotic interactions lead to a stable body plan? How do lichen architectures develop and grow? To address these questions, we use meta-omics, microscopy, and cell biology tools. Our ultimate goal is to study lichen symbiosis as a case of complexity arising in evolution. The lab opens in April 2026, stay tuned for updates!

entry.title: Official first day of the lab

link.title: Official first day of the lab

entry.content: As of today, the lab is officially open!
entry.summary: As of today, the lab is officially open!

entry.title: MetaLichenLab at EMBL symbiosis conference
entry.content: Gulnara presented her work on lichen secreted proteins at the EMBL Symposium “The cellular mechanics of symbiosis: sensing friend from foe” in Heidelberg, Germany. It was great to learn about great diversity of symbioses across the tree of life and different ways of studying them.
entry.summary: Gulnara presented her work on lichen secreted proteins at the EMBL Symposium “The cellular mechanics of symbiosis: sensing friend from foe” in Heidelberg, Germany. It was great to learn about great diversity of symbioses across the tree of life and different ways of studying them.

entry.title: Website is live
entry.content: Lab website is now live! The lab itself is coming April 2026
entry.summary: Lab website is now live! The lab itself is coming April 2026
